era: Hip-Hop. E=5 -- new city energy. Self-released. Tacoma origin with Seattle connections. FIRST NON-SEATTLE ORIGIN in the S36 engine. Tacoma enters as second city. Bridge figure between 253 and 206. Tenth degree in hip-hop DJ/producer era (134-143).

location.formative: Tacoma hip-hop scene. Hilltop neighborhood. Port city identity. I-5 corridor connection to Seattle. The 253 area code culture.
location.career: Spaceman Scotty. Tacoma origin. Seattle connections. Self-released. Bridge figure between Tacoma and Seattle hip-hop scenes. The first non-Seattle origin in the S36 engine.

members.name: Spaceman Scotty
members.note: Tacoma origin. First non-Seattle origin in S36 engine. Self-released. Bridge figure between Tacoma (253) and Seattle (206). Connects to Seattle hip-hop scene while maintaining Tacoma identity.

associated_musicians.name: 206 Zulu
associated_musicians.note: Previous degree. The collective's regional scope is made explicit by Spaceman Scotty's Tacoma origin.

associated_musicians.name: DJ Sounds
associated_musicians.note: Pioneer energy. Temporal origin (1983) parallels geographic origin (Tacoma).

associated_musicians.name: DJ B-Mello
associated_musicians.note: DJ era start. Ten degrees of hip-hop documentation (134-143) now spanning two cities.

theory_nodes.concept: First non-Seattle origin -- Tacoma enters the engine; geographic expansion from one city to multi-city Puget Sound ecosystem; the 253 meets the 206; I-5 corridor as cultural artery
examples: Degree 143: Tacoma enters. First non-Seattle city.
253 (Tacoma) and 206 (Seattle) area codes as cultural geography.
30 miles on I-5. Distinct cities, interconnected scenes.
theory_nodes.description: Tacoma enters. First non-Seattle origin. Geographic expansion.

theory_nodes.concept: Bridge figure -- Tacoma roots with Seattle connections; synthesizing two city identities; the I-5 corridor artist who belongs to the region rather than one city
examples: Tacoma identity is primary. Not a Seattle artist in Tacoma.
Active in Seattle scene. Performs at Seattle venues.
Self-released: consistent across both cities' independent scenes.
Stylistic synthesis: Tacoma grit + Seattle experimentation.
theory_nodes.description: Bridge figure. Tacoma to Seattle. Regional identity.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-released model -- no label, direct artist-to-audience; digital platforms + community distribution; the most independent form of independence
examples: Community: hand-off at shows, social media, networks.
Consistent across both cities' underground scenes.

cross_references.relationship: PREVIOUS DEGREE! 206 Zulu (142, regional collective) and Spaceman Scotty (143, Tacoma origin). The collective's regional scope is made explicit by the Tacoma entry.
PIONEER PARALLEL! DJ Sounds (141, temporal origin -- 1983) and Spaceman Scotty (143, geographic origin -- Tacoma). Both represent origins.
DJ ERA SPAN! Ten degrees (134-143). Now spanning two cities. The era's geographic scope expands.
NEXT DEGREE! Jazz Rap enters. Ishmael Butler connection. The hip-hop era shifts genre at 144.
